classroom.meaning: A piece of furniture with a flat surface where a student sits to work
classroom.example: I keep my pencils, crayons, and notebooks inside my wooden desk.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A desk is like a table where you sit and do your schoolwork.
classroom.sentence: Please put your books on top of your desk.

classroom.meaning: A piece of furniture with a seat, back, and legs for one person to sit on
classroom.example: My blue chair has four legs and a comfortable seat for sitting all day.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A chair is where you sit at your desk or at a table.
classroom.sentence: Push your chair under your desk when you stand up.

classroom.meaning: A piece of furniture with a flat top and legs, used for activities
classroom.example: Four students share one big table for art projects and group work.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A table is a flat surface where you can work, eat, or play.
classroom.sentence: Gather around the table for our science experiment.

classroom.meaning: A shiny white board that you write on with dry-erase markers
classroom.example: The teacher writes math problems on the big whiteboard and we solve them.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A whiteboard is where the teacher writes. You use special markers on it.
classroom.sentence: Come to the whiteboard and write the answer.

classroom.meaning: A dark green or black board that you write on with chalk
classroom.example: The teacher uses white chalk on the chalkboard and erases with an eraser.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A chalkboard is a dark board you write on with chalk. It can be dusty!
classroom.sentence: Write the word 'cat' on the chalkboard with chalk.

classroom.meaning: Another name for a whiteboard, used with dry-erase markers
classroom.example: Each student has a small marker board to practice spelling words.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A marker board is the same as a whiteboard. You write and wipe away.
classroom.sentence: Write your answer on your mini marker board and hold it up.

classroom.meaning: A board on the wall where you pin papers, artwork, and announcements
classroom.example: My drawing of a cat is on the bulletin board for everyone in class to see.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A bulletin board shows off your art and important school news.
classroom.sentence: Look at the bulletin board to see the schedule.

classroom.meaning: A board made of cork where you can pin things with pushpins
classroom.example: The corkboard has reminders, lunch menus, and pictures of our class pet.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A corkboard is made of cork so you can stick pins in it to hold papers.
classroom.sentence: Pin your permission slip to the corkboard.

classroom.meaning: A piece of furniture with horizontal shelves for holding books
classroom.example: The classroom bookshelf has many stories about animals, adventures, and friends.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A bookshelf holds all the classroom books so you can borrow them.
classroom.sentence: Return the book to the bookshelf when you are done.

classroom.meaning: A soft floor covering made of woven fabric
classroom.example: We sit on the colorful carpet during story time and morning meeting.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A carpet is a soft rug on the floor where you can sit comfortably.
classroom.sentence: Find your spot on the carpet and sit criss-cross applesauce.

classroom.meaning: A small carpet or floor covering
classroom.example: The reading corner has a soft round rug with alphabet letters on it.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A rug is like a small carpet that makes the floor softer.
classroom.sentence: Take off your shoes before you step on the rug.

classroom.meaning: An opening in the wall that has glass and lets in light and air
classroom.example: I look out the window and see birds in the tree and clouds in the sky.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A window is glass you can see through. It brings sunshine into the room.
classroom.sentence: Open the window to let fresh air come in.

classroom.meaning: A movable barrier used to close off an entrance to a room or building
classroom.example: We line up by the door when it's time for recess, lunch, or going home.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A door is how you go in and out of a room. Knock before you open it!
classroom.sentence: Close the door quietly so we don't disturb the other class.

classroom.meaning: The vertical surface that encloses or divides a room
classroom.example: The walls of our classroom are covered with posters, art, and vocabulary words.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: Walls are the sides of a room. They hold up the ceiling.
classroom.sentence: Hang your painting on the wall with tape.

classroom.meaning: The bottom surface of a room that you walk on
classroom.example: The floor is clean because the janitor mopped it after school yesterday.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: The floor is what you walk on in a room. Keep it clean!
classroom.sentence: Pick up the paper from the floor.

classroom.meaning: The top inside surface of a room above your head
classroom.example: The ceiling has lights, a fan, and sometimes hanging decorations for holidays.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: The ceiling is the top of the room above your head.
classroom.sentence: Look at the ceiling to see the stars we hung up.

classroom.meaning: A device that produces brightness so you can see
classroom.example: The teacher turns on the lights when it is cloudy and dark outside.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: Lights help you see when the room is dark or the sun goes away.
classroom.sentence: Please turn off the lights when we leave the room.

classroom.meaning: A portable light that stands on a desk or table
classroom.example: The reading corner has a small lamp that makes it cozy to read books.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A lamp is a light that you can move around. It sits on a desk or table.
classroom.sentence: Turn on the lamp when it gets dark outside.

classroom.meaning: An electronic machine that stores, processes, and shows information
classroom.example: We use the computer to play learning games, type stories, and research animals.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A computer is a machine that helps you learn, play games, and find information.
classroom.sentence: Log in to the computer with your username and password.

classroom.meaning: A set of keys or buttons used to type information into a computer
classroom.example: I use the keyboard to type my name and write sentences for my story.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A keyboard has letters and numbers you press to type on a computer.
classroom.sentence: Place your fingers on the home row keys.

classroom.meaning: A small device you move with your hand to control a computer cursor
classroom.example: I move the mouse to point to the game I want to play, then click the button.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A computer mouse helps you point and click on things on the screen.
classroom.sentence: Click the mouse to open the program.

classroom.meaning: The flat display surface of a computer, tablet, or TV where images appear
classroom.example: The computer screen shows pictures, words, and videos for us to learn from.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: The screen is the part you look at. It shows you everything.
classroom.sentence: Don't touch the screen with dirty fingers.

classroom.meaning: A machine that projects images and videos onto a large screen or wall
classroom.example: The teacher shows educational videos on the projector so everyone can see.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A projector makes pictures and movies big on a screen for the whole class.
classroom.sentence: Turn off the lights so we can see the projector better.

classroom.meaning: A large white surface where projector images are displayed
classroom.example: The pull-down screen covers the whiteboard when the teacher uses the projector.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A projector screen is where you watch movies or lessons from the projector.
classroom.sentence: Pull down the screen so the class can see the video.

classroom.meaning: A container for garbage, waste, and things you throw away
classroom.example: I throw my apple core, banana peel, and used paper in the trash can.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A trash can holds things you throw away. Keep our classroom clean!
classroom.sentence: Walk to the trash can and throw away your snack wrapper.

classroom.meaning: A container for items that can be reused like paper, plastic, and glass
classroom.example: We put empty water bottles, paper, and cardboard in the blue recycling bin.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A recycling bin is for things that can be made into new things.
classroom.sentence: Is this paper trash or recycling? Put it in the recycling bin.

classroom.meaning: A small bag, box, or pouch that holds writing tools and supplies
classroom.example: My pencil case has a zipper and holds ten pencils, an eraser, and a sharpener.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A pencil case keeps your pencils, erasers, and sharpeners all in one place.
classroom.sentence: Take out your pencil case and find a sharp pencil.

classroom.meaning: Small open storage compartments for each student's belongings
classroom.example: I put my backpack, lunchbox, and coat in my cubby every morning.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: Cubbies are little shelves where you keep your things at school.
classroom.sentence: Hang your coat in your cubby.

classroom.meaning: A small hook on the wall for hanging coats and jackets
classroom.example: Each student has a coat hook with their name written above it.
classroom.kid_friendly_explanation: A coat hook is where you hang your jacket so it doesn't get lost.
classroom.sentence: Hang your backpack on your coat hook.
